1: Give ear unto my voice, when I cry unto thee.
2: And And the lifting up of my hands as as the evening sacrifice.
3: Keep the door of my lips.
4: To practise wicked works with men that work iniquity: And let me not eat of their dainties.
5: For yet my prayer also shall be shall be in their calamities.
6: They shall hear my words; for they are sweet.
7: As when one cutteth and cleaveth wood wood upon the earth.
8: In thee is my trust; leave not my soul destitute.
9: Keep me from the snares which which they have laid for me, and the gins of the workers of iniquity.
10: Let the wicked fall into their own nets, whilst that I withal escape.
